title: Best VPN for Streaming 2026: Which Services Actually Unblock Netflix and More
description: The best VPN for streaming in 2026 must unblock Netflix, Disney Plus, BBC iPlayer and more. Here are the top performers based on real testing.
content:
Streaming services have gotten much better at detecting and blocking VPNs. In 2026, many free and cheap VPN providers are blocked by Netflix, Disney Plus, Amazon Prime Video, and BBC iPlayer. Only a handful of premium services consistently bypass these blocks. Here is what actually works.

The number one requirement for a streaming VPN is consistent unblocking. The VPN must have a large enough server network and rotate IP addresses frequently enough to stay ahead of streaming platform blocklists. Speed is the second requirement: anything below 50 Mbps on a connected server will cause buffering on 4K streams.

NordVPN remains the top choice for streaming in 2026. Its SmartPlay technology routes DNS queries through servers that are specifically optimized for unblocking streaming services. It reliably unblocks Netflix (including US, UK, and JP libraries), Disney Plus, BBC iPlayer, Hulu, and Amazon Prime Video. The server network of 6,000 plus servers across 111 countries gives it the depth to maintain clean IP addresses. Speed on European and US servers consistently exceeds 200 Mbps, which is more than enough for 4K.

ExpressVPN is the fastest VPN tested in 2026 and is reliable for streaming. Its Lightway protocol delivers excellent speeds on all server locations, and it successfully unblocks the major streaming platforms. The main downside is price: at around 12 dollars per month (or 8 dollars on annual), it is the most expensive mainstream option. If speed is the priority and price is secondary, ExpressVPN is the choice.

Surfshark is the best value option. At around 2.50 dollars per month on a two-year plan, it consistently unblocks Netflix and Disney Plus and offers unlimited simultaneous connections. The speed is lower than NordVPN or ExpressVPN, but still adequate for HD streaming. For families or users with many devices, Surfshark is hard to beat.

CyberGhost has dedicated streaming servers clearly labeled by the service they unblock (Netflix US, BBC iPlayer, etc.). This makes it the easiest option for non-technical users who just want to click and stream. It works reliably for the labeled services, though it struggles more than NordVPN with less common streaming platforms.

What to avoid: free VPNs are almost universally blocked by streaming services. The business model of a free VPN requires monetizing user data or displaying ads, and the limited server infrastructure means the same IP addresses are used by thousands of users, making them easy to block. Providers like ProtonVPN's free tier limit bandwidth significantly, making them unsuitable for video.

A note on legality: using a VPN to access content outside your region may violate the streaming service's terms of service. It is generally not illegal in most countries, but services can and do terminate accounts they detect doing this. In practice, terminations for VPN use are rare, but the risk exists.
